dailyJudgementSnippet: Anthropic wins the 2026-04-27 HN sentiment snapshot with the only positive score (+0.068), helped by clear praise for Claude CLI/Claude Code usage [E7][E8], despite notable complaints about Sonnet/Opus pricing [E5][E6]. Google Gemini was near neutral but slightly negative, OpenAI was more negative, and Microsoft Copilot was strongly negative amid pricing and billing backlash.

evidence.summary: The commenter calls the two parties "evil," which includes OpenAI in the deal breakup. The rest of the comment is mainly about Microsoft/Google AI spam, but the opening line is clearly negative toward OpenAI.
Calls OpenAI’s announcements "cringe" and says they went too hard on hype.
Calls their AGI framing a business move and says they are 'making up their stories,' implying dishonesty and lack of real science.
The commenter says GPT 5.4mini is 'even worse' and '~18 times more expensive now,' which is a clear negative judgment about OpenAI's model pricing/value.
The commenter directly blames Anthropic for raising Sonnet and Opus prices and calls the company’s actions theft, expressing clear anger over cost increases.
The comment calls '27x for Opus' 'genuinely shocking' and says it feels like paying a 'GitHub tax,' indicating strong dissatisfaction with Anthropic's pricing.
Directly says they now use the Claude CLI exclusively and are very pleased with the results, which is clearly favorable toward Anthropic/Claude.
The commenter praises Claude Code directly, saying they use it and that anything else they tried was significantly inferior, with only one exception.

The post reports Gemini-3-flash-preview's benchmark score as 47.8% and contrasts it with a higher score for the OSS agent (65.2%), implying underperformance relative to the benchmark result. The tone is mostly factual rather than explicitly critical.

Speaker says they haven’t had great experiences with Gemini for coding, citing poor command selection and not following idiomatic Go or codebase patterns. They note the added skill/version hasn’t helped much.
Directly compares Gemini unfavorably to another model and calls it 'quite useless,' indicating strong negative sentiment toward Gemini.
Calls Gemini 3.1 Pro a good model, though slow, and says Flash has been underappreciated.
The comment condemns GitHub Copilot’s pricing/billing change as a major reduction in value for annual subscribers, calling it unreasonable and suggesting legal/regulatory action.
The commenter says the price multiplier increase is ridiculous and that they have set a reminder to cancel their Copilot subscription, indicating strong dissatisfaction with Microsoft Copilot.
Describes GitHub Copilot as a 6-9x price increase, which is strongly unfavorable toward the product/business change.
Calls GitHub Copilot the most expensive LLM subscription and says any other subscription gives more usage per dollar.
